texto: We need to send our words out in the direction we want them to go. In other words, we need to start talking victory when we’re staring at defeat. We need to start talking healing when we’re feeling sick. We need to start blessing and prosperity when we don’t have anything. We need to speak about marching when we feel like quitting.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jentezen Franklin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “We need to send our words out in the direction we want them to go. In other words, we need to sta…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspiracional, motivational pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e termino.

frase_original: We need to send our words out in the direction we want them to go. In other words, we need to start talking victory when we’re staring at defeat. We need to start talking healing when we’re feeling sick. We need to start blessing and prosperity when we don’t have anything. We need to speak about marching when we feel like quitting.
